overview: ZED Certification (Zero Defect Zero Effect) is an extensive drive by the Ministry of MSME to create awareness amongst MSMEs about Zero Defect Zero Effect practices and motivate and incentivise them for ZED Certification while also encouraging them to become MSME Champions. The scheme aims to enhance MSME competitiveness, make them sustainable, and transform them into National and International Champions. It is implemented in two phases: Phase 1 for Manufacturing MSMEs with UDYAM Registration and Phase 2 for inclusion of Service Sector MSMEs. The scheme provides financial assistance/subsidy for obtaining ZED Certification at different levels (Bronze, Silver, Gold) and includes additional incentives like a joining reward of Rs. 10,000/- and support for handholding, technology upgradation, and surveillance assessments.
objectives: Encourage and enable MSMEs for manufacturing of quality products using latest technology, tools & to constantly upgrade their processes for achievement of high quality and high productivity with the least effect on the environment
Develop an Ecosystem for ZED Manufacturing in MSMEs, for enhancing competitiveness and enabling exports
Promote adoption of ZED practices and recognising the efforts of successful MSMEs
Encourage MSMEs to achieve higher ZED Certification levels through graded incentives
Increase public awareness on demanding Zero Defect and Zero Effect products through the MSME Sustainable (ZED) Certification
Identify areas to improve upon, thereby assisting the Government in policy decisions and investment prioritization
eligibility: All MSMEs registered with the UDYAM registration portal (of the MoMSME) will be eligible to participate in MSME Sustainable (ZED) Certification and avail related benefits/incentives. Any number of Units registered under one Udyam Registration can apply for subsidy under this Scheme. Each Unit (under one Udyam Registration) will need to apply for Certification separately to avail subsidy/benefits/incentives.
benefits: Joining reward of Rs. 10,000/- for ZED Certification (once, within 1 year of taking the ZED Pledge); Subsidy on cost of certification: Micro Enterprises: 80%, Small Enterprises: 60%, Medium Enterprises: 50%; Additional subsidy of 10% for MSMEs owned by Women/SC/ST Entrepreneurs OR MSMEs in NER/Himalayan/LWE/Island territories/aspirational districts; Additional subsidy of 5% for MSMEs which are also a part of the SFURTI OR Micro & Small Enterprises - Cluster Development Programme (MSE-CDP) of the Ministry; Financial assistance for handholding support (up to Rs. 5 Lakhs per MSME: up to Rs. 2 Lakhs for handholding and up to Rs. 3 Lakhs for technology upgradation) with subsidy structure: Micro Enterprises: 80%, Small Enterprises: 60%, Medium Enterprises: 50%, plus additional 10% for Women/SC/ST or special area MSMEs and 5% for SFURTI/MSE-CDP; Financial assistance for surveillance assessment: Silver Level: Rs. 10,000/-, Gold Level: Rs. 20,000/- with subsidy: Micro Enterprises: 80%, Small Enterprises: 60%, Medium Enterprises: 50%; 100% subsidy for women-owned MSMEs on cost of certification (as per addendum dated 11.11.2023); Free MSME KAWACH (WASH Certification) for ZED registered MSMEs; Assistance for obtaining international certifications like CE for international market access.
financial_support: Financial assistance/subsidy is provided for obtaining ZED Certification at Bronze, Silver, and Gold levels. The cost of certification is: Bronze: Rs. 8,000, Silver: Rs. 32,000, Gold: Rs. 72,000 (as per original guidelines) or amended to Bronze: Rs. 10,000, Silver: Rs. 40,000, Gold: Rs. 90,000 (as per Amendment_ZED_guideline_2.0_17.9.2024.pdf). Subsidy on cost of certification: Micro Enterprises: 80%, Small Enterprises: 60%, Medium Enterprises: 50%. Additional subsidy: 10% for MSMEs owned by Women/SC/ST Entrepreneurs OR MSMEs in NER/Himalayan/LWE/Island territories/aspirational districts. Additional subsidy: 5% for MSMEs which are also a part of the SFURTI OR Micro & Small Enterprises - Cluster Development Programme (MSE-CDP) of the Ministry. A limited-purpose joining reward of Rs. 10,000/- is offered to each MSME once they take the ZED Pledge, to be utilized within 1 year for ZED Certification (Bronze, Silver, Gold). For handholding support, a provision of up to Rs. 5 Lakhs per MSME is available: up to Rs. 2 Lakhs for handholding support and up to Rs. 3 Lakhs for technology upgradation, with subsidy structure: Micro Enterprises: 80%, Small Enterprises: 60%, Medium Enterprises: 50%, plus additional 10% for Women/SC/ST or special area MSMEs and 5% for SFURTI/MSE-CDP. For surveillance assessment: Silver Level: Rs. 10,000/-, Gold Level: Rs. 20,000/-, with subsidy: Micro Enterprises: 80%, Small Enterprises: 60%, Medium Enterprises: 50%. Women-owned MSMEs are eligible for 100% subsidy on cost of certification (effective from 11.11.2023).
application_process: 1. Register on the ZED portal (https://zed.msme.gov.in/) by providing Udyam Registration Number and associated mobile number. 2. Validate Udyam registration through API. 3. Provide valid Email ID for receiving ZED ID, Password, and Pledge Certificate. 4. Take the ZED Pledge online. 5. After registration, login with ZED ID and password. 6. Apply for desired ZED Certification Level (Bronze, Silver, or Gold) through the dashboard. 7. Download the ZED MSME mobile application from Play Store (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.qci.zed.msme) or App Store. 8. Provide GST Number and TAN Number as applicable. 9. Opt to avail the Joining Reward of Rs. 10,000/- (if applicable), which will be deducted from the total cost of certification. 10. Proceed to payment of the MSME contribution (certification fee minus any joining reward applied). 11. Select payment option from the list and make payment via payment gateway. 12. After successful payment, submit Basic Information and Documents via mobile application: capture MSME Representative Selfie and Site Exterior Photographs, and upload required documents. 13. Assessor will verify the information and documents submitted. 14. In case of non-conformity, the applicant MSME will be given an opportunity to close the non-conformity(ies) within a defined time period (30 days for Bronze, 60 days for Silver/Gold surveillance, etc.). 15. After successful verification, the ZED Certificate and Assessment Report will be issued and sent to the applicant's email ID and made available on the applicant dashboard.
required_documents: Mobile Number associated with Udyam Registration
Basic Information and Documents (as per certification level requirements)
deadlines: Rolling basis — no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted year-round.
contact_details: Email: zed.support@qcin.org | Phone: Mr. Shivam Chaubey: 9773770346 | Working days: Mon-Fri, 9:00 AM - 5:30 PM | Address: Ministry of MSME, Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises, Kartavya Bhawan - 03, Kartavya Path, New Delhi - 110001, India
caveats: The joining reward of Rs. 10,000/- is valid only for 1 year after taking the ZED Pledge and can be used only once while applying for a ZED Certification (Bronze, Silver, Gold).

If the joining reward is used for Bronze Certification, the effective cost to MSME becomes zero; for Silver or Gold, the reward amount is adjusted in the Certification Cost and subsidy applies on the balance amount.
The Fee for Certification or Handholding (Consultancy) is prescribed in the scheme guidelines; payment shall be made only through online mode as defined in the user manuals.
No online or offline agency, institution, assessor, or consultant is authorised to charge any fee or money for registration (ZED Registration is Free of Cost and paperless).
Surveillance assessment is mandatory for Silver and Gold Certified MSMEs: Surveillance 1 between 12 to 18 months, Surveillance 2 between 24 to 30 months after date of certification.
The Certification shall be suspended/withdrawn if the MSME fails to apply for or does not successfully complete Surveillance Assessments.
In case of withdrawal, the MSME will have to apply fresh by paying the entire Assessment/Certification Fee.
The financial support, incentives, benefits & other provisions contained in the scheme are valid till March 31, 2026 (as per Guidelines 2022).
For women-owned MSMEs, 100% subsidy on cost of certification is effective from 11.11.2023, and the additional subsidy provision of 10% and 5% is inapplicable for them.

MSMEs may opt for upgrading their Certification Level at any point of time before the expiry of validity of their existing ZED Certification; cost and subsidy will remain the same for the applied Level.
